briefs.title: Council to Consider Liquor Licence for Solo Karaoke on Kingsway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a recommendation about a liquor licence for Solo Karaoke at 102-6462 Kingsway. This follows an investigation into previous allegations. Council will decide whether to support the business's liquor primary licence, which allows a business to sell alcohol as its main activity.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it could affect the business's liquor licence.
briefs.source_body: 7.1 LLA #25-12 - 102-6462 KINGSWAY (SOLO KARAOKE) - UPDATE 1. LLA 25-12 102-6462 Kingsway 2026-03-10.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - LLA 25-12 - Sketch (102-6462 Kingsway) 2026-03-10.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Council Report LLA 25-12 - 102-6462 Kingsway (Solo Karaoke) dated November 25, 2025 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To summarize the findings of the investigation related to previous allegations, and to provide Council with a recommendation to support the subject liquor primary licence. LLA 25-12 102-6462 Kingsway 2026-03-10.pdf Attachment 1 - LLA 25-12 - Sketch (102-6462 Kingsway) 2026-03-10.pdf Attachment 2 - Council Report LLA 25-12 - 102-6462 Kingsway (Solo Karaoke) dated

briefs.title: Council to Consider Metro 2050 Amendments: Concerns from Other Mayors
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider sending a letter to the Metro Vancouver Board. The letter would share concerns about proposed changes to Metro 2050. Metro 2050 is a plan for how the region will grow and develop in the coming years. The Mayors of Surrey, Langley, and Delta have raised concerns about the proposed changes.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about Metro 2050 amendments and their potential impact on your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 7.2 COMMENTS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENT OPTIONS IN RESPONSE TO REQUEST BY THE MAYORS OF SURREY, LANGLEY TOWNSHIP AND DELTA 1. Comments on Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to Request by the Mayors 2026-03-10.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Letter from MVRD Board, dated January 20, 2026 - Proposed Metro 2050 Amendmen Options 2026-03-10.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Letter to MVRD Board, dated March 10, 2026 - Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to forward the attached comment letter to the Metro Vancouver Board conveying concerns about the four proposed Metro 2050 amendment options. Comments on Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to Request by the Attachment 1 - Letter from MVRD Board, dated January 20, 2026 - Proposed Metro 2 Attachment 2 - Letter to MVRD Board, dated March 10, 2026 - Proposed Metro 2050

briefs.title: Council to Vote: BC Hydro Statutory Right-of-Way on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider granting BC Hydro a Statutory Right-of-Way (SRW) on a section of 4111 Norland Avenue. An SRW gives BC Hydro the right to use the land for installing, operating, and maintaining equipment. Council will vote on whether to approve this SRW at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body: 7.3 STATUTORY RIGHT OF WAY FOR BC HYDRO - 4111 NORLAND AVENUE (COMMUNITY SAFETY BUILDING) 1. Statutory Right-Of-Way - 4111 Norland Avenue 2026-03-10.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Sketches 1 and 2 - 4111 Norland Avenue 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant a Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 4111 Norland Avenue to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of works. Statutory Right-Of-Way - 4111 Norland Avenue 2026-03-10.pdf Attachment 1 - Sketches 1 and 2 - 4111 Norland Avenue 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Applying for UBCM Grant for Development Approvals
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider applying for a grant from the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM). The grant is part of the Local Government Development Approvals Program. If approved, the grant would fund improvements to the City's development approval processes.
briefs.action_required: No action required. This is for informational purposes only.
briefs.source_body: 7.4 GA - APPLICATION TO UBCM LOCAL GOVERNMENT DEVELOPMENT APPROVALS PROGRAM GRANT FUNDING (2026) 1. GA-Application to UBCM LGDAP 2026 Grant Funding 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Local Government Development Approvals funding program. GA-Application to UBCM LGDAP 2026 Grant Funding 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote on IT Ticketing Software Licences
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for IT ticketing software licences. This software helps the city manage and track IT service requests. Council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the contract will allow the city to continue using this software.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 CA - IT TICKETING SOFTWARE LICENSES 1. CA - IT Ticketing Software Lincenses 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for IT Ticketing Software Licences. CA - IT Ticketing Software Lincenses 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Changes to Burnaby's Community Grant Policy and Application
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's Community Grant Policy and the related application process. The goal is to update the rules and forms for these grants. Council will vote on whether to approve these changes.
briefs.action_required: Review the draft policy and application to understand potential changes to grant eligibility.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Draft Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will soon review the draft Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan. This plan, called "Making Life Better," will guide the city's decisions about parks, recreation programs, and cultural activities. Council is expected to discuss how the plan will affect Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the draft plan to see how it might impact your business.
briefs.source_body: 8.2 PRCC - DRAFT PRC PLAN 1. PRCC - Draft PRC Plan 2026.03.10.pdf 2. PRCC - Draft PRC Plan 2026.03.10.pdf Attachment 1 - Draft Making Life Better PRC Plan 2026.03.10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Tourism Burnaby Support for Urban Forest Conference
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a request from Tourism Burnaby. Tourism Burnaby wants the City's support to bid for the 2028 Canadian Urban Forest Conference. Council's decision would be in principle, meaning it shows general support but isn't a final commitment.
briefs.action_required: No action required. Watch for updates on the Council's decision.

briefs.title: Council to Consider New Parking Spaces, Payment System in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a plan to create more off-street parking. The plan involves using funds from the Off-Street Parking Reserve Fund. Council is set to vote on starting a rezoning process and a study to see if new parking spaces are possible at 4301/4305 and 4171 Pender Street. This could also involve a "payment in-lieu" system, where developers can pay a fee instead of providing parking spaces themselves.
briefs.action_required: Monitor council decisions regarding parking development and payment changes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
briefs.source_body: 8.4 PDC - PROPOSED OFF-STREET PARKING SPACES - PAYMENT IN-LIEU 1. PDC - Proposed Off-Street Parking Spaces - Payment In-Lieu REVISED_ - 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to access the Off-Street Parking Reserve Fund and initiate rezoning and a feasibility study to support the development of new off-street parking spaces at 4301/4305 and 4171 Pender Street. PDC - Proposed Off-Street Parking Spaces - Payment In-Lieu REVISED_ - 2026-03-10

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the status of the Burnaby Mountain Safety Reserve Program for 2025. This program collects money from developers in the area to fund projects that improve safety and access on Burnaby Mountain. The report gives an update on how the money was used in 2025.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Gaming Reserve Status Update as of December 31, 2025
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review an update on the city's Gaming Reserve. This reserve holds money from gaming revenues, and the update will detail how much money is in the reserve and how it was spent as of December 31, 2025. Council will discuss the report at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is an informational update.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Receive Update on Civic Projects in February 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will get an update on significant civic projects in February 2026. This is for informational purposes, so Council can stay up-to-date on the progress of these projects. No decisions will be made at this time.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review information on the 'Project Plan-People-Play'. This plan relates to opening new recreation facilities in Burnaby. The plan's details are not included in this document.
briefs.action_required: No action is required at this time; this is an information update.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to receive update on Sexually Exploited Youth Task Force
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an update on the Burnaby-New Westminster Task Force on Sexually Exploited and At-Risk Youth. The update will cover the Task Force's activities. It will also include results from a survey on combatting online sexual exploitation.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Fire Department Status Update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the Fire Department's performance and activities up to December 2025. The report gives an overview of the department's operations, including emergency response times, training programs, and community outreach. Council will discuss the report's findings and consider any necessary actions or changes to improve fire safety and services in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to Consider RCMP Performance Report for August–November 2025
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the RCMP's performance report for August to November 2025. The report gives an update on policing operations. Council will also review the Annual Performance Plan quarterly report, which covers July to September 2025. These reports help Council understand how the RCMP is performing in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for awareness.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Changes to Burnaby's Parking and EV Charging Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw. The proposed bylaw would affect the rules for the public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. Council is set to vote on the changes at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the council's decision, as it may impact your business operations.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider amending the city's fees and charges bylaw. The proposed change would set fees for using Burnaby's public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. Council will vote on the proposed bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. The proposed bylaw would affect enforcement related to public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The goal is to update the rules for dealing with violations at these charging stations.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed on the decision, as it may affect parking and enforcement related to EV charging.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: New Zoning Districts in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's zoning rules. A proposed bylaw would create five new zoning districts. These changes aim to align with the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP). The OCP is a guide for future growth and land use in the city. Zoning bylaws control how land can be used.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed zoning changes to see if they affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 11.2.1 #14792 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 - Text Amendment 1. 14792. Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 Amendment No. 2, 2026 New Zoning Districts fin

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 50-Storey Rental Tower at Walker Ave. & Arcola St.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a zoning change for 7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St. A proposed bylaw would allow building a 50-storey rental apartment building. It also includes a 6-storey community hub with retail, office, and community space like a food bank. The plan includes about 480 rental apartments, with 96 offered at below-market rates.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council meetings for updates on the rezoning proposal.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 11.3.1 #14744 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2025 - REZ #24-20 (7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St.) 1. 14744 Rez 24-20 (7135 Walker and 7244 Arcola).ACD 2025 FINAL (FA).pdf 2. REZ 24-20 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: to establish development guidelines for the construction of a 50-storey residential rental tower with an adjoining 6-storey community hub inclusive of an auditorium, retail space, community serving uses (such as a food bank), and office space. The development proposal includes approximately 480 purpose-built rental units, including approximately 96 non-market rental units (Item 7.6., Administrative Report, Council April 22, 2025) Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development - March 10, 2026 14744 Rez 24-20 (7135 Walker and 7244 Arcola).ACD 2025 FINAL (FA).pdf REZ 24-20 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Rezoning for Still Creek Ave Self-Storage Facility
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a proposal to rezone 3944 and 3942 Still Creek Ave. The applicant wants to change a previously approved plan for a five-storey self-storage building with two underground parking levels. The new plan includes removing one parking level, changing vehicle access, raising the flood construction level, and building a six-storey self-storage facility.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council's decision, as it may affect local businesses.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 11.3.2 #14763 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 15, 2025 - REZ #25-05 (3944 and 3942 Still Creek Ave) 1. 14763 Rez 25-05 (3944 and 3942 Still Creek).ACD 2025 (FA).pdf 2. REZ 25-05 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: to amend previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #22-24 (Bylaw No. 14591), which received Final Adoption on January 28, 2025 and which permitted the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage facility with two levels of underground parking. The applicant is now proposing to remove one parking level and all self-storage uses from underground parkade, revise vehicular access, and elevate the building's flood construction level, resulting in a six-storey self-storage building (Item 7.6., Administrative Report, Council August 26, 2025) Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development - March 10, 2026 14763 Rez 25-05 (3944 and 3942 Still Creek).ACD 2025 (FA).pdf REZ 25-05 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning Amendment for 5889 Barker Ave — Additional Rental Housing
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to a previously approved rezoning plan for 5889 Barker Avenue. The proposal would allow for six more storeys of market rental housing on top of a planned six-storey non-market rental building. This would create a 12-storey rental building with a mix of rental rates.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision if you have interests near Barker Avenue.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 11.3.3 #14782 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 19, 2025 - REZ #25-15 (5889 Barker Avenue) 1. 14782 Rez 25-15.ACD 2025 (FA).pdf 2. REZ 25-15 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #17-40 (Bylaw No. 14181), which received Final Adoption of November 8, 2021, and which permitted the construction of a 43-storey residential strata apartment tower and a six-storey non-market rental building with underground parking. The proposed amendment seeks to permit an additional six storeys of market rental housing above the approved non-market rental building, resulting in a 12-storey rental building with a mix of rental rates (Item 8.11., PDC Report, Council October 28, 2025) Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development - March 10, 2026 14782 Rez 25-15.ACD 2025 (FA).pdf REZ 25-15 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Changes to Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw on Bear Spray
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on changes to the Business Licence Bylaw. A proposed bylaw amendment would affect how businesses in Burnaby regulate bear spray. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting. The aim is to update the rules around bear spray sales and storage.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your business licence.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Bylaw on Bear Spray Sales
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. The proposed bylaw would regulate the sale of bear spray in Burnaby. Council will vote on the changes at an upcoming meeting. The aim is to update rules around bear spray sales.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed on the Council's decision, as it may affect your business operations.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Changes to Burnaby Plumbing Fees
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's plumbing fees. These fees are part of the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Council is set to vote on Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026, which would change the fees. The exact changes to fees were not specified in the article.
briefs.action_required: Check the Council meeting agenda to learn about specific fee changes.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Changes to Burnaby Advisory Body Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the rules that guide its advisory bodies. The proposed bylaw, called "Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026," would amend the "Terms of Reference" for these advisory groups. The Terms of Reference are the rules that say how the advisory bodies must operate.

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Tenant Assistance for New Burnaby Developments
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider how to determine if tenants in buildings being redeveloped are eligible for the city's Tenant Assistance Policy (TAP). The proposal would apply existing rules to new rezoning applications. TAP is a program that provides support to tenants who are displaced due to redevelopment.
briefs.action_required: No action required at this time. Watch for the Council decision.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body: 14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 023 1. Attachment 1 - PDC - Determining TAP Eligibility 2026-03-10.pdf THAT staff be directed to apply the criteria, as outlined in Attachment 1 of this report (Option 1: Maintain Original Eligibility Date), to determine tenant eligibility for new rezoning applications, in respect of development sites with an in-stream rezoning application; and THAT the recommendation and Attachment 1 of the report be released to the Open Council meeting on March 10, 2026. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Attachment 1 - PDC - Determining TAP Eligibility 2026-03-10.pdf
